subject: Mathematics
solution:
1. 1. A straight line $\boldsymbol{L}$ passes through the point of intersection of 2 straight lines: $\left\{\begin{array}{l}\boldsymbol{L}_1: 4 \boldsymbol{x}+3 \boldsymbol{y}-1=0 \\ \boldsymbol{L}_2: 3 \boldsymbol{x}+4 \boldsymbol{y}-1=0\end{array}\right.$

  2. 2. The straight line $\boldsymbol{L}$ cuts the axes at $\boldsymbol{P}$ and $\boldsymbol{Q}$

  3. 3. To identify the locus of the mid-point of the segment $P Q$

  1. The point of intersection of $\boldsymbol{L}_1$ and $\boldsymbol{L}_2$ is $\boldsymbol{I}(\boldsymbol{\alpha}, \boldsymbol{\beta})$

  2. The mid-point of the segment $\boldsymbol{P Q}$ is $\boldsymbol{M}(\boldsymbol{h}, \boldsymbol{k})$

Analytical Study :

We first solve the pair of straight lines $\boldsymbol{L}_1$ and $\boldsymbol{L}_2$ to locate the point of intersection :

$$ \begin{aligned} & 4 x+3 y-1=0 \Rightarrow 12 x+9 y=3 \\ & 3 x+4 y-1=0 \Rightarrow 12 x+16 y=4 \end{aligned} \quad \Rightarrow \beta=\frac{1}{7} \Rightarrow \boldsymbol{\alpha}=\frac{1}{7} \Rightarrow \boldsymbol{I}\left(\frac{1}{7}, \frac{1}{7}\right) \ldots . . (i)$$

The straight line $\boldsymbol{L}$ passes through the point $\boldsymbol{I}$ identified by equation (i)

The equation of the straight line $\boldsymbol{L}$ in point slope form is written below :

$$ L:\left(y-\frac{1}{7}\right)=m\left(x-\frac{1}{7}\right) \equiv L: 7 m x-7 y=m-1 \equiv L: \frac{7 m x}{m-1}+\frac{7 y}{1-m}=1 \ldots . (ii)$$

The equation (ii) gives the straight line $\boldsymbol{L}$ in intercept form where the $\boldsymbol{X}$ and $\boldsymbol{Y}$ intercepts are observed below :

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \boldsymbol{P} & \boldsymbol{Q} \\ \hline\left(\frac{\boldsymbol{m}-1}{7 \boldsymbol{m}}, 0\right) & \left(0, \frac{1-\boldsymbol{m}}{7}\right) \\ \hline \end{array}\text { ..... Chart } 1 \end{aligned} $$

From Chart 1, the mid-point of the section $\boldsymbol{P Q}$ is derived applying mid-point formulae :

$$ M(h, k)=M\left(\frac{m-1}{14 m}, \frac{1-m}{14}\right) \equiv \begin{array}{|l|l|l|} \hline \text { Point } & h & k \\ \hline M(h, k) & \frac{m-1}{14 m} & \frac{1-m}{14} \\ \hline \end{array} \quad \ldots . \text { Chart } 2 $$

Chart 2 has the $\boldsymbol{X}$ and $\boldsymbol{Y}$ coordinate each as a function of $\boldsymbol{m}$ where we can eliminate $\boldsymbol{m}$ and mathematically trace the locus :

$$ \boldsymbol{k}=\frac{1-\boldsymbol{m}}{14} \Rightarrow \boldsymbol{m}=1-14 \boldsymbol{k} \Rightarrow \boldsymbol{h}=\frac{1-14 \boldsymbol{k}-1}{14(1-14 \boldsymbol{k})}=\frac{14 \boldsymbol{k}}{14(14 \boldsymbol{k}-1)}=\frac{\boldsymbol{k}}{(14 \boldsymbol{k}-1)} \Rightarrow 14 \boldsymbol{h} \boldsymbol{k}-\boldsymbol{h}=\boldsymbol{k} \Rightarrow \boldsymbol{h}+\boldsymbol{k}-14 \boldsymbol{h} \boldsymbol{k}=0 $$

The locus of the mid-point of the section $\boldsymbol{P Q}$ after replacement of the dummy variables is found to be the rectangular hyperbola $\boldsymbol{x}+\boldsymbol{y}-14 \boldsymbol{x} \boldsymbol{y}=0 $
title: JEE Main 2026 (Online) 8th April Evening Shift

subject: Mathematics
options: The lines are concurrent at the point $$\left( {{3 \over 4},{1 \over 2}} \right)$$
Each line passes through the origin
solution:
Equation of lines;

px + qy + r = 0   . . . . . (1)

Also given

3p + 2q + 4r = 0   . . . . . . (2)

divide equation (2) by 4, we get

$${3 \over 4}P + {2 \over 4}q + r = 0$$   . . . . (3)

By comparing (1) and (3) we get,

x = $${3 \over 4}$$ and y = $${2 \over 4}$$ = $${1 \over 2}$$

For any value of p,q and r, the equation of set of lines will pan through $$\left( {{3 \over 4},{1 \over 2}} \right)$$
title: JEE Main 2019 (Online) 9th January Morning Slot
